Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: paintings by material or technique [300033701], paintings (visual works) [300033618], visual works by material or technique [300191091], visual works (works) [300191086], Visual Works (hierarchy name) [300179869], Visual and Verbal Communication (hierarchy name) [300264552], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Refers to two-dimensional works of art, usually on a paper support, to which pigment suspended in water is applied with a brush to create an image or design. Includes paintings using gouache, which is not technically watercolor paint. Watercolors are variously classified as drawings or paintings in collections.

Source.Source_ID: 2000047814/Mayer, Dictionary of Art Terms and Techniques (1991)

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Se refiere a obras de arte bidimensionales, usualmente sobre un soporte de papel, al cual se le aplica conuna brocha pigmento suspendido en agua para crear una imagen o diseño.

Verwijst naar tweedimensionale kunstwerken die bestaan uit in water gesuspendeerd pigment (waterverf) dat met een penseel is aangebracht op een gewoonlijk papieren drager om een beeld of ontwerp te scheppen.
Bezieht sich auf ein zweidimensionales Kunstwerk, gewöhnlich auf Papier, auf die ein in Wasser aufgelöster Farbstoff mit einem Pinsel aufgetragen wird, um ein Bild oder ein Muster zu schaffen.

Refere-se às obras de arte bidimensionais, geralmente sobre papel, nas quais o pigmento suspenso em água é aplicado com um pincel para criar uma imagem ou desenho. Inclui pinturas usando guache que não é tecnicamente tinta aquarela. Aquarelas são classificadas como desenhos ou pinturas nas coleções. Para trabalhos criados exclusivamente com tinta aquarela, use "aquarela (pintura)".

Source.Source_ID: 2000026701/Mayer, Dictionary of Art Terms and Techniques (1969)
2000047814/Mayer, Dictionary of Art Terms and Techniques (1991)

Term_Source.Page: Comment - NALLON - 3/94 - wanted British English spelling as UK descriptor.

Source.Source_ID: 2000017031/Zinkham and Parker, Descriptive Terms for Graphic Materials (1986)

Term_Source.Page: Candidate comment -- CCA -- 4/93. Want Canadian spelling as lead-in.
